column1: Summary
Account Visibility (ACV)
Promise-to-Pay / Payment-Plan Journey (PTP)
Routing and Exception Handling (REH)
Access and Verification (AV)
Reporting and Audit Trail (RAT)
Real-time Ingestion Pipeline for Core Legacy Database
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a backend integration service, I want to establish a real-time data ingestion pipeline that replicates delinquent account data from the 20-year-old legacy_db into the CRM data cache so that agents have immediate access to baseline account financial data.

* *Business Value:* Forms the foundational data layer for the centralized workspace, eliminating the need for agents to manually hop into the legacy terminal.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* a change event (e.g., account flags, balance updates) occurs in legacy_db, *When* the replication service captures the event, *Then* it must upsert the target record in the CRM cache within {{<5}} seconds.
** *Given* a network latency spike or transmission failure, *When* the pipeline attempts to update the CRM, *Then* it must log an error and retry the transaction up to 3 times via a dead-letter queue before alerting administrators.
* *Dependencies:* legacy_db read-access permissions and CDC (Change Data Capture) configuration.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Performance testing must prove zero degradation on core legacy server memory loads during peak daytime operation hours.

column1: Local Team Spreadsheet Data Extraction & Ingestion Engine
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a data migration tool, I want to parse, sanitize, and ingest historical contact notes and custom fields from local team-specific spreadsheet trackers into the centralized CRM cache.

* *Business Value:* Eliminates hidden institutional context locked within local Excel files, addressing the issue where accessing a customer's history depends on manual spreadsheet extraction.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* a collection of historical team tracking spreadsheets, *When* the data parser runs, *Then* it must correctly map historical text fields to standard CRM conversation string parameters based on the unique Account ID.
** *Given* duplicate or conflicting history logs exist between spreadsheets, *When* executing the import, *Then* resolve using the latest timestamp sequence and append an [Imported-Excel] trace tag to the record.
* *Dependencies:* TS-01 (Baseline data cache mapped to unique Account IDs).
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Assumes data formatting across local team trackers is standardized enough to be programmatically tokenized via regex or python scripts.

column1: Shared Email Interaction Aggregation Pipeline
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As an integration middleware service, I want to monitor the shared collections Outlook inbox and parse incoming/outgoing emails to attach them directly to the corresponding CRM customer profile.

* *Business Value:* Prevents communication fragmentation, addressing the pain point where agents re-contact customers who were already promised callbacks because the email tracker is separated from the core database.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* an email is received from or sent to a debtor, *When* the email hook intercepts the message, *Then* it must search the CRM database using the debtor's registered email address or Account ID found in the subject header.
** *Given* a match is found, *When* updating the CRM, *Then* embed the entire plain-text string, timestamp, and attachments directly into the account's centralized interaction timeline view.
* *Dependencies:* Outlook Exchange API connectivity
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Inbound email text containing credit card or payment card details must be masked before saving to satisfy PCI-DSS compliance regulations.

column1: Telephony Interaction Log Sync Integration
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a communication integration layer, I want to capture phone call events, durations, and call disposal statuses from the telephony framework and stream them directly into the core CRM timeline view.

* *Business Value:* Builds a single source of truth for all historical customer contacts, lowering Average Handle Time (AHT) by eliminating verbal memory checks and phone log hunting.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* an agent completes a customer call, *When* they select a call wrap-up disposition code, *Then* the telephony module must emit a JSON payload containing the call metadata to the CRM webhook.
** *Given* the CRM receives the payload, *When* verified, *Then* update the customer profile with a call event marker within $\le 2$ seconds of the agent dropping the call.
* *Dependencies:* Telephony provider webhook support
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Call records must associate cleanly even if multiple callers are registered under a single account group.

column1: Single Screen Customer Timeline Interface Construction
Local Spreadsheet Write-Permission Deprecation (Sunset Policy)
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a systems security administrator, I want to transition shared team tracking spreadsheets to read-only status and enforce systemic locks on the day of CRM deployment.

* *Business Value:* Mitigates the risk of "shadow spreadsheets" and data mutation, permanently forcing all user data entry to occur inside the unified system of record.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* the scheduled CRM go-live window, *When* the cutover script is initiated, *Then* revoke active user write-permissions across all network file-shares for the listed collection Excel sheets.
** *Given* a user attempts to open a legacy Excel sheet after launch, *When* the system intercepts the request, *Then* present an instructional popup window redirecting them to the newly launched CRM environment.
* *Dependencies:* Active Directory group ownership controls.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Read access to the historical tracking sheets must remain live for at least 90 days to assist team leaders with back-check auditing.

column1: Custom Team "Views" UI Configurations for Managers
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a frontend engineer, I want to build custom, filtered grid dashboard displays within the CRM interface that closely mirror the retired spreadsheet visual layouts for Team Leaders.

* *Business Value:* Eases change management frictions and prevents behavioral resistance, allowing managers to execute performance tracking without off-system data hoarding.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* a Team Leader logs into the CRM platform, *When* they access their department dashboard, *Then* render team performance and workload telemetry metrics using a tabular, row-and-column layout mimicking Excel formats.
** *Given* the grid dashboard interface view, *When* a manager edits an account detail line item, *Then* update the change back to the central database while forcing a mandatory change tracking context dropdown reason log.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Field structures within these customized grid views must be restricted by user roles to maintain compliance safety.

column1: Write-Protected PTP Registry Component Creation
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a database architect, I want to create a secure, write-protected database registry table explicitly dedicated to defining, tracking, and logging Promise-to-Pay (PTP) commitments.

* *Business Value:* Replaces manual text notes with formal data fields, solving the problem where payment promises are treated identically to confirmations and historical tracking maps are lost.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* an agent negotiates an arrangement, *When* they commit a PTP to the system, *Then* generate a unique PTP ID and require four strict telemetry inputs: Target Amount, Due Date, Installment Interval, and Associated Account ID.
** *Given* an active PTP record in the database registry, *When* an application user attempts to modify or delete a historical transaction entry line item, *Then* block the mutation and throw an unauthorized error code.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* All PTP registry entry rows must contain automated, non-editable audit tracking hashes to verify record integrity.

column1: Overnight Staging Database and Overnight Bank Data Ingestion Setup
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a data engineer, I want to create a detached overnight data staging database that receives and processes daily bank transaction statements safely away from the active system layer.

* *Business Value:* Minimizes performance strain and database timeouts on the vulnerable 20-year-old core legacy architecture.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* an automated daily bank transactional statement update file, *When* the script fires at *02:00 AM*, *Then* ingest the bank ledger lines directly into the temporary detached overnight staging database environment.
** *Given* the data ingestion run is active, *When* loading the files, *Then* verify file formatting integrity check balances against batch file headers before opening data tables for downstream matching.
* *Dependencies:* Secure FTP connection to bank clearinghouses.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Data written to the overnight staging layer must follow automated data lifecycle retention schedules and flush once updates are finalized.

column1: Automated Asynchronous PTP Payment-Matching Algorithm Execution
column26:
* *User Story Statement:* As a backend processing service, I want to execute automated payment-matching algorithms during low-traffic windows to reconcile banking statement files against active PTP ledger parameters.
* *Business Value:* Delivers an automated PTP fidelity check mechanism, eliminating the need for finance teams to manually reconcile accounts every month.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* active registry rows, *When* the matching application runs at *02:30 AM* inside the staging database container, *Then* cross-reference inbound bank transaction amounts against PTP installment rows using the matching unique Account ID.
** *Given* an active payment confirmation matches a payment amount row exactly, *When* finalized, *Then* log an asynchronous success validation timestamp and mark the target PTP line item record state as _Settled_.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* The matching logic must include configurable multi-day grace windows to account for standard banking processing holidays.

h3.

column1: Rule-Driven Exception Routing for Broken PTP Agreements
column26:
* *User Story Statement:* As a workflow execution component, I want to immediately adjust status classifications and re-route accounts to urgent manual queues whenever the payment-matching engine identifies a broken arrangement.
* *Business Value:* Captures the 14% missed follow-up data leakage, ensuring accounts never sit unmanaged or un-tracked in dead status queues for months at a time.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* the payment-matching algorithm determines a debtor failed to fulfill an installment payment obligation, *When* the grace window passes, *Then* change the account status immediately to _PTP_Broken_.
** *Given* an account transitions to a broken state status, *When* the script completes, *Then* automatically drop account collection freeze flags, push the account folder into the urgent manager manual queue list, and stage an outreach event flag.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Staged outreach triggers must conform strictly to upstream client contact rules to prevent compliance exposure.

column1: Perimeter Algorithmic Ingestion Gate Component Creation
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a backend validation application, I want to inspect specific metadata tags on incoming delinquent records at the portfolio boundary to determine appropriate processing pathways.

* *Business Value:* Establishes the rule-driven gateway framework needed to separate trivial portfolios from specialized ones, allowing managers to optimize the allocation of human capital.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* a collection list file load contains newly delinquent account rows, *When* the file hits the gateway ingestion layer, *Then* the parsing algorithm must examine four data field parameters: Days Past Due (DPD), overall balance scale, core product type, and consumer compliance history flag records.
** *Given* an individual record contains invalid, corrupted, or missing data field values across the required inspection parameters, *When* verified, *Then* halt processing for that specific entry row and push the exception file row into an administrative data correction workspace queue.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* The metadata triage gate engine must process account file volumes of $\ge 100,000$ files without running up memory exhaustion crashes.

column1: Fast-Track Ingestion Engine for Straightforward Portfolios
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As an automated routing component, I want to isolate the 38% share of early-stage, low-complexity cases and pass them directly into a digital fast-track queue to bypass human review steps.

* *Business Value:* Reduces investigation duration and processing cycles from an 18-minute manual lookup loop down to a 10-minute automated assignment sequence, boosting operational throughput.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* a case file passes through the algorithmic ingestion gate, *When* its metadata profiles match early-stage delinquency attributes (e.g., low DPD, standard product type), *Then* categorize the file folder index under the _Straightforward_Digital_ tracking queue flag.
** *Given* an account sits in the fast-track queue, *When* finalized, *Then* unlock direct programmatic communication visibility permissions to enable automated SMS or email dispatch triggers.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Accounts assigned to the digital fast-track pipeline must still remain accessible to manager oversight screens for quality check sampling.

column1: Hard-Route Exclusion Filtering Logic for Auto Finance Portfolios
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As an engineering process rule component, I want to identify Auto Finance accounts at the ingestion gate and bypass all automated outreach channels to force a manual agent assignment pathway.

* *Business Value:* Enforces systemic operational boundaries, ensuring that collateralized asset configurations representing 15.5% of active portfolios are managed strictly by specialists to limit repossession liabilities.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* an account file payload reaches the ingestion engine stage, *When* the product type parameter maps to an asset-backed _Auto_Finance_ identifier string code, *Then* immediately bypass all automated digital outreach triggers.
** *Given* the Auto Finance account routing bypass, *When* executed, *Then* apply a strict hard-lock _Manual_Specialist_Review_ flag tag to the record file and push it exclusively to the senior operations collateral specialist assignment queue group.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Automated communication suppression must be write-protected so that no manual configuration change can accidentally enable mass automated texts to this portfolio.

column1: Hard-Route Exclusion Filtering Logic for Late-Stage Delinquency Portfolios
column26:
* *User Story Statement:* As an engineering process rule component, I want to isolate accounts in late-stage delinquency or designated on legal watch at the boundary layer to prevent automated communication fires.
* *Business Value:* Protects the brand from intense regulatory and legal non-compliance exposures by routing severe 15.9% volume late-stage accounts away from hands-off automated paths.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* a case payload processes through the perimeter validation block, *When* data values indicate Late Delinquency parameters (e.g., severe DPD ranges) or match active _legal_watch_ tags, *Then* terminate all automated workflow execution sequences immediately.
** *Given* the late-stage routing bypass termination, *When* confirmed, *Then* push the account file record into the specialist litigation team portfolio list queue for individual manual review and correspondence verification.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Vulnerable customer hardship assessments are completely excluded from any automated resolution path and must follow this manual routing mechanism.

column1: Agent Single Sign-On (SSO) & Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Identity Provisioning
column26:
*User Story Statement:* As a systems security service, I want to integrate the core CRM workspace with Legacy-Trust Bank’s centralized Active Directory (AD) identity provider using OAuth 2.0/SAML protocols so that access is restricted to verified collection personnel.

* *Business Value:* Ensures a secure perimeter and mitigates data leakage risk for the bank's portfolio of over 100,000 sensitive recovery cases.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* an internal collections agent navigates to the CRM workspace URL, *When* they submit their corporate credentials, *Then* authenticate the user against the AD ledger and issue an encrypted, time-bound session token ($\le 8$ hours).
** *Given* a successful authentication attempt, *When* enforcing security thresholds, *Then* trigger a secondary hardware token MFA challenge before granting access to the unified interface.
* *Dependencies:* Corporate Active Directory / Identity Provider API access.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Session timeouts must be strictly enforced at 15 minutes of inactivity to safeguard frontline terminal screens.

column1: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) Permitting Matrix Engine
column26:
* *User Story Statement:* As a system administrator, I want to establish an RBAC engine that dynamically filters editable fields, data views, and manual override paths based on specific user role groupings (Agent, Team Leader, Compliance Auditor).
* *Business Value:* Protects vulnerable portfolios from unauthorized automated or manual changes, directly reducing operational compliance risk.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* a logged-in user has the {{Collections_Agent}} role, *When* they open an account file, *Then* grant them write permissions for standard negotiation tools but completely hide system administrative configuration options.
** *Given* a logged-in user has the {{Compliance_Auditor}} role, *When* auditing a case history timeline, *Then* render all transaction histories as strictly read-only, disabling all balance modification hooks.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* Any configuration update to the RBAC matrix tables must be flagged for secondary system manager approval.

column1: Context-Mandated Dropdown Note capture & Schema Setup
column26:
* *User Story Statement:* As a database engineer, I want to configure the CRM update schema to enforce mandatory dropdown status justification logging for any manually initiated account state modifications.
* *Business Value:* Replaces poor manual notes with standard definitions, providing clean audit telemetry for management teams who previously stopped running formal reports entirely due to poor data quality.
* *Acceptance Criteria:*
** *Given* an agent manually changes an account state flag (e.g., shifting an account from "Active" to "Disputed"), *When* they trigger the save action, *Then* halt processing and present a modal window requiring a selection from a predefined dropdown list of standardized justification codes.
** *Given* a user confirms the justification selection, *When* executing the save command, *Then* commit the status update string, the user’s corporate ID, a timestamp, and the justification token to the account's internal event table history.
* *Notes or Assumptions:* This metadata drop-down strategy acts as the Phase 1 bridge to manage compliance auditing safely without writing heavy automated tracking scripts directly onto the fragile legacy database infrastructure.
